pullRequest.body: Refactor UserRepository: rename findByEmail to findByEmailAddress, change findAll to support pagination with Page<User>

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find services calling UserRepository to assess downstream impact
Find controllers calling UserRepository to verify API layer impact
Find services calling UserRepository to assess downstream impact

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Renamed from findByEmail -- update UserService and AuthController
public Optional<User> findByEmailAddress(String email) {
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The method findByEmail(String email) was renamed to findByEmailAddress(String email) on line 37, but UserService.getUserByEmail() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/UserService.java and AuthController.login() in src/main/java/com/acme/controller/AuthController.java still call findByEmail(email). This will cause a compilation error, and at runtime via reflection or dynamic proxies, the method will not be found, throwing NoSuchMethodError.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed findByEmail to findByEmailAddress breaks UserService and AuthController which still call the old method name

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Return type changed from List<User> to Page<User> -- update AdminDashboardService
public Page<User> findAll(Pageable pageable) {
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The return type of findAll() was changed from List<User> to Page<User> and now requires a Pageable parameter (line 52), but AdminDashboardService.getTotalUserCount() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/AdminDashboardService.java still calls findAll() without arguments and assigns the result to List<User>. This will cause a compilation error since findAll() no longer exists without parameters, and even if it compiled, Page<User> cannot be assigned to List<User>.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Changed findAll() return type from List<User> to Page<User> breaks AdminDashboardService which calls .size() on the result

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The findById method returns null instead of Optional.empty() on line 31 when no user is found. Callers using .orElseThrow() or .isPresent() on the returned Optional will get a NullPointerException instead of the expected behavior, since they expect an Optional that is either present or empty, never null.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: findById returns null instead of Optional.empty() causing NullPointerException for callers using orElseThrow

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.existingCode:
PreparedStatement stmt = connection.prepareStatement(
                "SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY " + sortField);
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The findAllSorted method on line 78 builds the SQL query using string concatenation: "SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY " + sortField. The sortField parameter is not validated against a whitelist of allowed column names. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL via the sortField parameter (e.g., "name; DROP TABLE users--"), leading to SQL injection.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: SQL injection in findAllSorted via unvalidated sortField string concatenation in ORDER BY clause

pullRequest.body: Restructure ConfigurationManager from static utility methods to singleton instance pattern

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find services calling ConfigurationManager to assess downstream impact
Find services calling ConfigurationManager to assess downstream impact
Find services calling ConfigurationManager to assess downstream impact
Find configuration consuming ConfigurationManager to verify settings propagation

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The ConfigurationManager was changed from static methods (ConfigurationManager.get(key)) to an instance-based singleton pattern (ConfigurationManager.getInstance().getValue(key)) on lines 18-27. EmailService.sendEmail() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/EmailService.java, PaymentGateway.processPayment() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/PaymentGateway.java, and NotificationService.notifyUser() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/NotificationService.java all still call ConfigurationManager.get(key) as a static method. Since get() no longer exists as a static method, these calls will cause a compilation error or NoSuchMethodError at runtime.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Changed from static ConfigurationManager.get(key) to instance getValue(key) breaks EmailService, PaymentGateway, and NotificationService

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The static method getInt(key) which returned an int by parsing the string config value was removed entirely. ConnectionPoolConfig.getMaxPoolSize() and ConnectionPoolConfig.getMinIdle() in src/main/java/com/acme/config/ConnectionPoolConfig.java still call ConfigurationManager.getInt(key). Since this method no longer exists, these calls will fail with a compilation error or NoSuchMethodError at runtime, preventing connection pool initialization.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Removed getInt(key) method breaks ConnectionPoolConfig which relied on automatic int parsing

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The reload() method on lines 47-57 builds a new HashMap and then replaces the config field reference on line 56 with this.config = newConfig. This is not thread-safe: concurrent readers may see the old config reference and read stale values, or see a partially constructed HashMap if the JVM reorders the writes. In a multi-threaded server environment, this can lead to intermittent configuration errors. The config field should be declared as volatile, or a ConcurrentHashMap with atomic replacement should be used.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Configuration reload not thread-safe -- concurrent readers can see partially loaded config during reload

pullRequest.body: Refactor DateTimeUtils from java.util.Date to java.time API (LocalDate, DateTimeFormatter)

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find services calling DateTimeUtils to assess downstream impact
Find services calling DateTimeUtils to assess downstream impact
Find services calling DateTimeUtils to assess downstream impact

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.existingCode: public static String formatDate(LocalDate date, String pattern) {
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Parameter type changed from Date to LocalDate -- update InvoiceGenerator and AuditLogger
public static String formatDate(LocalDate date, String pattern) {
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The formatDate method signature was changed from formatDate(Date date, String pattern) to formatDate(LocalDate date, String pattern) on line 10, but InvoiceGenerator.generateInvoice() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/InvoiceGenerator.java and AuditLogger.logAction() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/AuditLogger.java still pass java.util.Date objects. This will cause a compilation error since java.util.Date is not compatible with java.time.LocalDate. The callers must be updated to convert Date to LocalDate or use LocalDate.now() directly.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Changed formatDate parameter from Date to LocalDate breaks InvoiceGenerator and AuditLogger which pass java.util.Date

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The parseDate methods return type was changed from java.util.Date to java.time.LocalDate on lines 15 and 19, but ImportService.importRecord() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/ImportService.java assigns the result to a Date variable. This will cause a compilation error since LocalDate cannot be assigned to Date. The caller must be updated to use LocalDate or convert the result.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Changed parseDate return type from Date to LocalDate breaks ImportService which assigns result to a Date variable

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The daysBetween method on line 24 calls ChronoUnit.DAYS.between(start, end) but does not validate for null arguments. If either start or end is null, a NullPointerException is thrown with no meaningful error message. The method should validate inputs and throw an IllegalArgumentException with a descriptive message.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: daysBetween throws NullPointerException when either date argument is null -- no input validation

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The toEpochMillis method on line 37 hardcodes ZoneOffset.UTC when converting LocalDate to epoch milliseconds. For users in negative UTC offsets (e.g., America/New_York at UTC-5), calling toEpochMillis(LocalDate.of(2024, 1, 15)) returns the epoch time for midnight UTC, which corresponds to the previous day (Jan 14, 7pm EST). This causes off-by-one-day bugs. The method should use ZoneId.systemDefault() or accept a ZoneId parameter.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: toEpochMillis hardcodes UTC timezone causing off-by-one-day errors for users in negative UTC offsets

pullRequest.body: Refactor EventBus from string-based event types with EventHandler interface to class-based generics with Consumer<Event>

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find event listeners consuming EventBus to verify event contract compatibility
Find event listeners consuming EventBus to verify event contract compatibility
Find services calling EventBus to assess downstream impact
Find event listeners consuming EventBus to verify event contract compatibility

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The subscribe method signature was changed from subscribe(String eventType, EventHandler handler) to subscribe(Class<? extends Event> eventClass, Consumer<Event> handler) on line 17. OrderEventListener in src/main/java/com/acme/listener/OrderEventListener.java calls eventBus.subscribe("order.created", this) and InventoryEventListener in src/main/java/com/acme/listener/InventoryEventListener.java calls eventBus.subscribe("inventory.updated", this), both passing String and EventHandler. These calls will fail with a compilation error since the method now expects Class and Consumer parameters.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Changed subscribe(String, EventHandler) to subscribe(Class, Consumer) breaks OrderEventListener and InventoryEventListener

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.existingCode:
public void publish(Event event) {
        List<Consumer<Event>> list = handlers.get(event.getClass());
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The publish method signature was changed from publish(String eventType, Object payload) to publish(Event event) on line 28. CheckoutService.checkout() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/CheckoutService.java still calls eventBus.publish("order.created", Map.of(...)) with a String and Object payload. This will fail with a compilation error since publish now expects a single Event parameter.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Changed publish(String, Object) to publish(Event) breaks CheckoutService which passes string event type and payload

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.existingCode: public void subscribe(Class<? extends Event> eventClass, Consumer<Event> handler) {
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// WARNING: EventHandler interface removed -- update AuditEventHandler to use Consumer<Event>
public void subscribe(Class<? extends Event> eventClass, Consumer<Event> handler) {
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The EventHandler interface was removed from the codebase in favor of Consumer<Event> (line 7 imports Consumer, and subscribe on line 17 accepts Consumer<Event>). AuditEventHandler in src/main/java/com/acme/listener/AuditEventHandler.java implements EventHandler, which no longer exists. This will cause a compilation error. AuditEventHandler must be rewritten to implement Consumer<Event> instead.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Removed EventHandler interface breaks AuditEventHandler which implements it

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The publish method on lines 28-35 iterates over the handler list directly with a for-each loop. If a handler modifies the subscription list during event processing (e.g., calls unsubscribe to remove itself or subscribe to add a new handler), a ConcurrentModificationException will be thrown because the underlying ArrayList is being modified while being iterated.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Event dispatch iterates handler list directly -- ConcurrentModificationException if handler modifies subscriptions

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.existingCode: private final Map<Class<? extends Event>, List<Consumer<Event>>> handlers;
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// Consider walking the class hierarchy in publish() to support polymorphic dispatch
private final Map<Class<? extends Event>, List<Consumer<Event>>> handlers;
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The handlers map uses Class<? extends Event> as the key (line 11), but due to Java's type erasure, subscribing with a specific event subclass (e.g., OrderEvent.class) stores handlers under the exact runtime class. When publish() on line 29 looks up handlers using event.getClass(), it will only find handlers registered for the exact class, not superclasses. This means subscribe(Event.class, handler) will not receive OrderEvent instances, breaking polymorphic event handling that worked with the old string-based approach.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Generic type erasure breaks polymorphic event handling -- handlers registered for Event.class won't receive subclass events

pullRequest.body: Refactor HttpClientWrapper from Apache HttpClient to Java 11 java.net.http.HttpClient

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find services calling HttpClientWrapper to assess downstream impact
Find services calling HttpClientWrapper to assess downstream impact
Find hooks depending on HttpClientWrapper to verify consumer impact

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The get(String url) method return type was changed from CloseableHttpResponse (Apache) to java.net.http.HttpResponse<String> (Java 11) on line 21. ApiGatewayService.fetchData() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/ApiGatewayService.java and HealthCheckService.isServiceHealthy() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/HealthCheckService.java both assign the result to CloseableHttpResponse and call Apache-specific methods like .getStatusLine().getStatusCode(). These will fail with a compilation error since the return type is now incompatible. The callers must use .statusCode() and .body() instead.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Changed get() return type from Apache CloseableHttpResponse to java.net.http.HttpResponse breaks ApiGatewayService and HealthCheckService

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.existingCode:
public HttpResponse<String> post(String url, Map<String, String> headers, String body)
            throws Exception {
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The post method parameter order was changed from post(String url, String body, Map<String, String> headers) to post(String url, Map<String, String> headers, String body) on line 37. WebhookClient.sendWebhook() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/WebhookClient.java calls httpClient.post(url, payload, headers) passing the String body as the second argument and Map headers as the third. With the new signature, the body string will be treated as the headers Map parameter, causing a compilation error since String cannot be assigned to Map<String, String>.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Changed post() parameter order from (url, body, headers) to (url, headers, body) breaks WebhookClient which passes body before headers

codeSuggestions.label: bug
codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The connection timeout is set to Duration.ofMillis(0) on line 16, which means zero milliseconds (effectively infinite or implementation-dependent). If the target host is unreachable, the HTTP client will hang indefinitely waiting for a connection to be established. This blocks the calling thread forever. The timeout should be set to a reasonable value like 5000 or 10000 milliseconds.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Connection timeout set to 0 (infinite) -- requests to unreachable hosts will hang forever
